testcase.name: internal
testcase.error:
def worker_internal_error( self, node: WorkerController, formatted_error: str ) -> None: """ pytest_internalerror() was called on the worker.
pytest_internalerror() arguments are an excinfo and an excrepr, which can't be serialized, so we go with a poor man's solution of raising an exception here ourselves using the formatted message. """ self._active_nodes.remove(node) try: > assert False, formatted_error E AssertionError: Traceback (most recent call last): E File "C:\Users\AILeo\miniconda3\envs\rqa\lib\site-packages\coverage\sqlitedb.py", line 115, in _execute E return self.con.execute(sql, parameters) # type: ignore[arg-type] E sqlite3.OperationalError: no such table: file E E During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: E E Traceback (most recent call last): E File "C:\Users\AILeo\miniconda3\envs\rqa\lib\site-packages\coverage\sqlitedb.py", line 120, in _execute E return self.con.execute(sql, parameters) # type: ignore[arg-type] E sqlite3.OperationalError: no such table: file E E The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception: E E Traceback (most recent call last): E File
